scenario: ELIGIBLE — All criteria met but values are EXACTLY at threshold (boundary test). No margin of safety.

civil_engineer.name: Er. Ashok Tiwari
civil_engineer.notes: Exactly 10 years — right at the minimum requirement

turnover.notes: Average is EXACTLY Rs. 5 Crore — right at the 'not less than Rs. 5 Crore' threshold. Technically passes (>= not >), but there is zero margin.

net_worth.notes: Positive but barely — just Rs. 10 Lakh. Technically passes the 'positive net worth' criterion.

similar_works.project: Construction of Type-II Quarters for CPWD, Bhopal
similar_works.notes: Value is EXACTLY Rs. 2 Crore — right at the minimum per-project threshold

similar_works.project: Office Renovation for State Bank of India, Indore
similar_works.notes: Just Rs. 5 Lakh above minimum threshold. SBI is a PSU, so qualifies as government.

similar_works.project: Guest House for MP State Govt, Jabalpur
similar_works.notes: Completed exactly 5 years before 31 March 2026 — right at the edge of the 5-year window. Date is 1 April 2021. Does 'last 5 years ending 31 March 2026' include projects completed on 1 April 2021? This is a boundary question.

iso_9001.notes: Just issued on 15 April 2026 — obtained very recently, just 15 days before submission deadline. Valid but new.

emd.in_favour_of: Commandant, Group Centre, CRPF, New Delhi

expected_verdict.overall: ELIGIBLE (but all values are at exact thresholds)

criteria.turnover: ELIGIBLE — Average exactly Rs. 5 Cr. 'Not less than' means >= so Rs. 5 Cr passes. But zero margin.
criteria.net_worth: ELIGIBLE — Positive (Rs. 10 Lakh). Barely, but the criterion is just 'positive'.
criteria.similar_works: ELIGIBLE (borderline) — 3 projects, all >= Rs. 2 Cr, all government. Third project's date (Apr 2021) is at the edge of the 5-year window — system should flag if unclear.
criteria.iso_9001: ELIGIBLE — Valid, though just issued. Tender says 'valid as on date of submission', which it is.

expected_verdict.notes: Boundary value test. Every value is at or near the exact threshold. Tests whether the system correctly applies >= (not >) and handles edge dates. The system should pass this bidder but may reasonably flag the tight margins for officer awareness.
